ops.title: semsearch: index-cache crash-safety via atomic multi-file swap
ops.description: Review finding I4 (verified 2026-07-22, deferred). store() writes chunks.json -> vectors.bin -> manifest.json; each write is atomic (temp+rename) but the 3-file SET is not, and nothing links them. A crash between the chunks.json and vectors.bin writes (user Ctrl-C on a long index) leaves NEW chunks + OLD vectors; loadPersisted checks only schema/model/content/chunker + len(chunks)==len(vectors) + dims consistency + per-file range contiguity vs the old manifest, so a count-preserving edit slips through -> chunk text and embedding disagree -> silently wrong rankings until that file changes again. Fix: stage all artifacts and atomically swap, or a shared generation nonce written into all three and validated in loadPersisted.

ops.text: A crash between cache-file writes cannot yield a loadable cache with mismatched chunks/vectors (generation nonce or atomic set-swap)
